name: Barbecue Chicken Skewers (The Best)
ingredients: 1 ½ lb (675 g) boneless, skinless chicken breast, cubed
6 green onions, cut into 1 ½-inch (4 cm) pieces (optional)
instructions: In a glass dish, combine the buttermilk and spices. Add the chicken. Season with salt and pepper. Cover and refrigerate for 24 hours.
In a large glass bowl, whisk together all of the ingredients. Cook in the microwave oven for 1 minute or until the sauce starts boiling. Set aside.
Preheat the grill, setting the burners to medium. Oil the grate.
Drain the chicken and pat dry slightly. Discard the marinade. Thread the chicken cubes onto flat metal skewers alternating with the green onions, if desired. Lightly oil the skewers.

Grill the skewers for 5 minutes on each side. Brush the chicken with half of the barbecue sauce and grill for a minute longer on each side or until the chicken is cooked through. Set aside on a plate and tent with foil. Let sit for 5 minutes.
Serve the chicken skewers with the remaining barbecue sauce. Serve with rice and green salad, if desired.

name: Vegetable Stew with Vegetarian Balls
ingredients: 1/4 cup (60 mL) soya or other milk
1/2 cup (125 mL) breadcrumbs or crumbled dry bread
1/4 cup (60 mL) whole wheat flour, lightly toasted
instructions: In a saucepan, simmer the vegetables for 30 minute in the water and soya sauce.
Meanwhile, prepare the balls by mixing together all the ingredients (except the bay leaves and salt) and shaping into small balls. Cook in an oiled frying pan.
Add the balls and bay leaves to the vegetables, season with salt to taste and cook together for another 30 minutes.

name: Chicken chow mein
ingredients: Salt and pepper from the mill
500g boneless and skinless chicken breasts, cut into strips
3 large carrots from Nantes, cut in julienne
1 small yellow onion, cut in half and in thin strips (1/2 cup)
1 red bell pepper, cut in half and in strips
1 1/2 cups bean sprouts, rinsed and drained
Salt and pepper from the mill
instructions: In a bowl, pour soy sauce and cornstarch. Mix well to dissolve. Add remaining ingredients for the sauce, season with salt and pepper and mix. Set aside.
In a bowl, add chicken and soy sauce then toss. Set aside.
In a large saucepan filled with boiling water, add the pasta and cook a little less than the time mentioned on the package. Pour into a colander, rinse with cold water and drain. Set aside.
In the same saucepan over medium heat, add canola oil followed by ginger, garlic, carrots and onion. Season with salt and pepper and cook for 4 to 5 minutes, stirring constantly. Add oil as needed.
Add chicken and pepper then stir. Cook for 5 to 6 minutes until chicken is almost cooked.
Add mushrooms and cabbage and continue cooking for 2 minutes.
Add the noodles, the sauce and the bean sprouts then season with salt and pepper. Cook for 2 minutes stirring, to warm and finish cooking the noodles. Garnish with slices of green onions and serve!

name: Apple Coffee Cake
ingredients: 1/4 cup white or apple cider vinegar
1 1/2 cups thinly sliced baking apples such as Granny Smith, Golden Delicious or Honey Crisps (about 3)
instructions: Preheat oven to 400 degrees and well grease a 9-inch square baking pan.
In a large mixing bowl, sift together the flour, sugar, baking soda, salt and cinnamon.
Cut in the shortening.
In a small bowl beat the egg, milk and vinegar together. Add to the flour mixture and stir until all the flour is moistened. This will be a sticky dough.
Spread into the baking pan and arrange the sliced apples on top of the batter.
Mix together the topping ingredients and sprinkle over the top.
Bake for 30 minutes or until cake is baked through.
Eat warm or at room temperature. Enjoy!

name: Baked Feta Pasta
ingredients: Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper
One 8-ounce block feta cheese, drained (see Cook’s Note)
Pinch crushed red pepper flakes, optional
1/4 cup fresh basil leaves, thinly sliced
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees F.

Toss the cherry tomatoes and olive oil with 1/4 teaspoon of salt and several grinds of black pepper in a medium bowl until combined. Transfer to a 2 1/2- to 3-quart baking dish, place the feta in the center of the tomatoes and season with a pinch of black pepper. Bake until the tomatoes have burst and the feta has softened, about 30 minutes. Raise the heat to 450 degrees F and continue to cook until the tomatoes and feta are golden brown, 10 to 15 minutes more.

Meanwhile, bring a large pot of generously salted water to a boil over high heat. Add the pasta and cook until al dente, about 13 minutes. Reserve 1/2 cup of the cooking water, then thoroughly drain the pasta.

As soon as the tomatoes and feta come out of the oven, stir in the garlic and red pepper flakes (if using). Use the back of a spoon to smash the tomatoes and feta into a smooth and creamy sauce (it’s okay if some of the oil isn’t fully integrated). Add the pasta and half the basil and toss until evenly coated. If the sauce is too thick, stir in some pasta water a couple tablespoons at a time. Taste and adjust the seasoning with salt and pepper. Serve topped with the remaining basil and a pinch of sea salt.

name: QUICHE AUX CHOUX DE BRUXELLES, PANCETTA & CHÈVRE
ingredients: 1 tasse de choux de Bruxelles, coupés en 4
1 abaisse de pâte brisée, maison ou du commerce
¾ de tasse de lait ou de crème 35%
1 tasse de fromage de chèvre, émietté
instructions: Préchauffer le four à 375F et placer la grille au centre du four. Tapisser une plaque à cuisson de papier parchemin.
Dans un bol, mélanger l’huile d’olive et les choux de Bruxelles puis assaisonner. Verser sur la plaque et enfourner pendant 20 minutes. Réserver.
Dans une casserole, fondre le beurre à feu moyen et cuire la pancetta pendant 10 minutes ou jusqu’à ce qu’elle soit légèrement croustillante. Réserver.
Sur un comptoir légèrement fariné, abaisser la pâte et foncer un moule à tarte de 9 pouces. Réserver.
Dans un grand bol, fouetter les oeufs avec le lait ou la crème et assaisonner. Verser le tout dans l’abaisse de pâte.

Garnir des choux de Bruxelles, de la pancetta et du fromage de chèvre. Enfourner pendant 30 minutes ou jusqu’à ce que le mélange d’oeufs soit bien pris. Laisser tempérer pendant 10 minutes avant de déguster.

name: Folks & Forks | Pappardelles champignons sauvages et Alfred le fermier
ingredients: 1 c. à soupe de thym frais
3 tasses de champignons sauvages au choix
½ tasse de vin blanc sec
½ tasse de fromage Alfred le fermier grand cru râpé + pour servir
½ tasse d’eau de cuisson des pâtes
instructions: Dans une poêle à feu moyen avec le beurre, faire revenir les échalotes française avec une pincée de sel jusqu’à ce qu’elles soient translucides, puis ajouter le thym.
Ajouter les champignons avec l’ail, puis faire revenir sans trop les remuer, jusqu’à ce qu’ils tombent et soit légèrement dorés.
Déglacer avec le vin blanc, puis laisser réduire pendant deux minutes.
Incorporez la crème et le fromage. Mélanger, puis laisser mijoter la sauce jusqu’à ce qu’elle soit bien onctueuse. Saler et poivrer.
Goûter, puis rectifier l’assaisonnement.
Dans une casserole d’eau salée, cuire les Pappardelles jusqu’à ce qu’elles soient al dente. Garder un peu d’eau de cuisson avant dégoûter.
Ajouter les pâtes à la sauce, puis incorporer un peu d’eau de cuisson.
Servir immédiatement avec encore un peu de fromage râpé.
